evidence_validation.result: all referenced relative source files exist; cited lines were read during audit
evidence_validation.note: Line references are relative to target_repo.

results.comment: BoringSSL encodes the DTLS handshake header with handshake_write_seq, increments it after each new handshake message, uses handshake_read_seq as next_receive_seq, discards past fragments, queues bounded future fragments, and preserves the sequence value when retransmitting stored outgoing messages.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; Section 4.2.6 CertificateVerify and Finished Messages
results.comparison_summary: requirement: must be present for message_seq under condition 'CertificateVerify and Finished hash calculations'; standard meaning: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; Section 4.2.6 CertificateVerify and Finished Messages; code behavior: BoringSSL encodes the DTLS handshake header with handshake_write_seq, increments it after each new handshake message, uses handshake_read_seq as next_receive_seq, discards past fragments, queues bounded future fragments, and preserves the sequence value when retransmitting stored outgoing messages.; conclusion: 满足.

results.related_state_or_step: next_receive_seq initialization for received message sequencing
results.comment: BoringSSL encodes the DTLS handshake header with handshake_write_seq, increments it after each new handshake message, uses handshake_read_seq as next_receive_seq, discards past fragments, queues bounded future fragments, and preserves the sequence value when retransmitting stored outgoing messages.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; Section 4.2.6 CertificateVerify and Finished Messages
results.comparison_summary: requirement: set to constant for message_seq under condition 'counter initialization'; standard meaning: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; Section 4.2.6 CertificateVerify and Finished Messages; code behavior: BoringSSL encodes the DTLS handshake header with handshake_write_seq, increments it after each new handshake message, uses handshake_read_seq as next_receive_seq, discards past fragments, queues bounded future fragments, and preserves the sequence value when retransmitting stored outgoing messages.; conclusion: 满足.

results.change_condition: in the case of a rehandshake for HelloRequest
results.comment: BoringSSL initializes and increments DTLS handshake message_seq for supported initial handshakes, but DTLS renegotiation is explicitly unsupported, so the HelloRequest rehandshake-specific message_seq=0 behavior is not implemented as an executable DTLS path.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; Section 4.2.6 CertificateVerify and Finished Messages
results.comparison_summary: requirement: set to constant for message_seq under condition 'in the case of a rehandshake for HelloRequest'; standard meaning: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; Section 4.2.6 CertificateVerify and Finished Messages; code behavior: BoringSSL initializes and increments DTLS handshake message_seq for supported initial handshakes, but DTLS renegotiation is explicitly unsupported, so the HelloRequest rehandshake-specific message_seq=0 behavior is not implemented as an executable DTLS path.; conclusion: 部分满足.

results.change_condition: in the case of a rehandshake for ServerHello
results.comment: The general handshake_write_seq counter can produce increasing message numbers, but BoringSSL rejects DTLS renegotiation traffic, so the rehandshake ServerHello message_seq=1 case is not a supported runtime behavior.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; Section 4.2.6 CertificateVerify and Finished Messages
results.comparison_summary: requirement: set to constant for message_seq under condition 'in the case of a rehandshake for ServerHello'; standard meaning: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; Section 4.2.6 CertificateVerify and Finished Messages; code behavior: The general handshake_write_seq counter can produce increasing message numbers, but BoringSSL rejects DTLS renegotiation traffic, so the rehandshake ServerHello message_seq=1 case is not a supported runtime behavior.; conclusion: 部分满足.

results.comment: BoringSSL defines DTLS1_MT_HELLO_VERIFY_REQUEST as handshake type 3, parses handshake msg_type from the DTLS header, and dispatches HelloVerifyRequest in the DTLS client state machine.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format
results.comparison_summary: requirement: set to constant for msg_type under condition 'Handshake body selection'; standard meaning: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; code behavior: BoringSSL defines DTLS1_MT_HELLO_VERIFY_REQUEST as handshake type 3, parses handshake msg_type from the DTLS header, and dispatches HelloVerifyRequest in the DTLS client state machine.; conclusion: 满足.

results.change_action: must be one of enumerated HandshakeType values
results.new_value: hello_request(0), client_hello(1), server_hello(2), hello_verify_request(3), certificate(11), server_key_exchange(12), certificate_request(13), server_hello_done(14), certificate_verify(15), client_key_exchange(16), finished(20)
results.comment: BoringSSL defines DTLS1_MT_HELLO_VERIFY_REQUEST as handshake type 3, parses handshake msg_type from the DTLS header, and dispatches HelloVerifyRequest in the DTLS client state machine.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format

results.comment: The second ClientHello after HelloVerifyRequest is generated by reusing ssl->s3->client_random and adding only the stored DTLS cookie.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; Section 4.2.6 CertificateVerify and Finished Messages

results.change_action: copy from client's ClientHello record sequence number
results.new_value: record sequence number in the ClientHello
results.comment: BoringSSL implements client-side parsing of HelloVerifyRequest, but the server implementation contains no HelloVerifyRequest generation/cookie-exchange path, so it cannot copy the ClientHello record sequence number into an outgoing HelloVerifyRequest.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.1 Record Layer; Section 4.1.2.1 MAC; Section 4.1.2.6 Anti-Replay; Section 4.2.4 Handshake Message Fragmentation and Reassembly
results.comparison_summary: requirement: copy from client's ClientHello record sequence number for sequence_number under condition 'Server sends HelloVerifyRequest'; standard meaning: RFC 6347 Section 4.1 Record Layer; Section 4.1.2.1 MAC; Section 4.1.2.6 Anti-Replay; Section 4.2.4 Handshake Message Fragmentation and Reassembly; code behavior: BoringSSL implements client-side parsing of HelloVerifyRequest, but the server implementation contains no HelloVerifyRequest generation/cookie-exchange path, so it cannot copy the ClientHello record sequence number into an outgoing HelloVerifyRequest.; conclusion: 不满足.

results.change_condition: after the initial ServerHello, once the server has created state
results.comment: BoringSSL tracks DTLS record numbers per epoch, initializes new write epochs at sequence 0, increments only after sealing, rejects replay/window-old records before decrypting, records sequence numbers after successful AEAD/MAC open, and uses epoch||sequence as the DTLS 1.2 AEAD/MAC sequence input.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.1 Record Layer; Section 4.1.2.1 MAC; Section 4.1.2.6 Anti-Replay; Section 4.2.4 Handshake Message Fragmentation and Reassembly

results.change_action: must equal / copied from another field
results.change_condition: to avoid sequence number duplication in case of multiple cookie exchanges
results.new_value: record sequence number in the ClientHello
results.comment: The RFC rule avoiding sequence-number duplication across multiple cookie exchanges depends on actually sending HelloVerifyRequest records. BoringSSL has no server-side HVR send path and only assigns normal fresh record numbers when sealing records.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.1 Record Layer; Section 4.1.2.1 MAC; Section 4.1.2.6 Anti-Replay; Section 4.2.4 Handshake Message Fragmentation and Reassembly

BoringSSL has no server-side HVR send path and only assigns normal fresh record numbers when sealing records.; conclusion: 不满足.

results.change_action: must not use for version negotiation judgment
results.comment: For received HelloVerifyRequest, BoringSSL parses the server_version field for syntax but does not use it for version negotiation; actual negotiated version is determined later from ServerHello, which matches the RFC client-side rule.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.1 Denial-of-Service Countermeasures; Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format
results.comparison_summary: requirement: must not use for version negotiation judgment for server_version under condition 'Client receives HelloVerifyRequest version value'; standard meaning: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.1 Denial-of-Service Countermeasures; Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; code behavior: For received HelloVerifyRequest, BoringSSL parses the server_version field for syntax but does not use it for version negotiation; actual negotiated version is determined later from ServerHello, which matches the RFC client-side rule.; conclusion: 满足.

results.change_action: must equal previously received HelloVerifyRequest server version
results.comment: For received HelloVerifyRequest, BoringSSL parses the server_version field for syntax but does not use it for version negotiation; actual negotiated version is determined later from ServerHello, which matches the RFC client-side rule.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.1 Denial-of-Service Countermeasures; Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format
results.comparison_summary: requirement: must equal previously received HelloVerifyRequest server version for server_version under condition 'Client receives ServerHello after HelloVerifyRequest'; standard meaning: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.1 Denial-of-Service Countermeasures; Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; code behavior: For received HelloVerifyRequest, BoringSSL parses the server_version field for syntax but does not use it for version negotiation; actual negotiated version is determined later from ServerHello, which matches the RFC client-side rule.; conclusion: 满足.

results.comment: The client can parse a HelloVerifyRequest.server_version field, but no BoringSSL DTLS server code emits HelloVerifyRequest, so the DTLS 1.2 server SHOULD-send-DTLS-1.0-HVR-version behavior is absent.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.1 Denial-of-Service Countermeasures; Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format
results.comparison_summary: requirement: set to constant for server_version under condition 'DTLS 1.2 server implementations send HelloVerifyRequest'; standard meaning: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.1 Denial-of-Service Countermeasures; Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; code behavior: The client can parse a HelloVerifyRequest.server_version field, but no BoringSSL DTLS server code emits HelloVerifyRequest, so the DTLS 1.2 server SHOULD-send-DTLS-1.0-HVR-version behavior is absent.; conclusion: 不满足.

results.comment: BoringSSL parses HelloVerifyRequest as uint16 server_version plus uint8-length-prefixed cookie on the client side, but because the server never generates the message, syntax support is receive-only.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.1 Denial-of-Service Countermeasures; Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format
results.comparison_summary: requirement: validated by syntax equivalence for server_version under condition 'HelloVerifyRequest structure definition'; standard meaning: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.1 Denial-of-Service Countermeasures; Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; code behavior: BoringSSL parses HelloVerifyRequest as uint16 server_version plus uint8-length-prefixed cookie on the client side, but because the server never generates the message, syntax support is receive-only.; conclusion: 部分满足.

results.change_action: must not assume not-DTLS-1.2 / must not assume eventual negotiation to DTLS 1.0
results.comment: For received HelloVerifyRequest, BoringSSL parses the server_version field for syntax but does not use it for version negotiation; actual negotiated version is determined later from ServerHello, which matches the RFC client-side rule.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.1 Denial-of-Service Countermeasures; Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format
results.comparison_summary: requirement: must not assume not-DTLS-1.2 / must not assume eventual negotiation to DTLS 1.0 for server_version under condition 'HelloVerifyRequest uses version 1.0'; standard meaning: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.1 Denial-of-Service Countermeasures; Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; code behavior: For received HelloVerifyRequest, BoringSSL parses the server_version field for syntax but does not use it for version negotiation; actual negotiated version is determined later from ServerHello, which matches the RFC client-side rule.; conclusion: 满足.

results.change_action: must equal value that would be used in ServerHello
results.comment: The requirement that a server use in HelloVerifyRequest the same version it would use in ServerHello cannot be exercised because BoringSSL does not implement server-side HelloVerifyRequest generation.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.1 Denial-of-Service Countermeasures; Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format
results.comparison_summary: requirement: must equal value that would be used in ServerHello for server_version under condition 'Server sends HelloVerifyRequest'; standard meaning: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.1 Denial-of-Service Countermeasures; Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; code behavior: The requirement that a server use in HelloVerifyRequest the same version it would use in ServerHello cannot be exercised because BoringSSL does not implement server-side HelloVerifyRequest generation.; conclusion: 不满足.

results.comment: The ClientHello writer reuses hs->session_id for the retransmitted DTLS ClientHello while adding hs->dtls_cookie.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; Section 4.2.6 CertificateVerify and Finished Messages
results.comparison_summary: requirement: must equal original ClientHello value for session_id under condition 'Client responds to a HelloVerifyRequest'; standard meaning: RFC 6347 Section 4.2.2 Handshake Message Format; Section 4.2.6 CertificateVerify and Finished Messages; code behavior: The ClientHello writer reuses hs->session_id for the retransmitted DTLS ClientHello while adding hs->dtls_cookie.; conclusion: 满足.

results.comment: BoringSSL TLS cipher registry contains AES, 3DES, AEAD, PSK, ECDHE-PSK and ChaCha20-Poly1305 suites but no RC4, KRB5, export RC4, or anonymous RC4 suites, so these DTLS-prohibited RC4 suites cannot be negotiated.

results.comment: BoringSSL defines DTLS1_2_VERSION as 0xfefd, emits DTLS record headers through dtls_record_version, and supplies record_version plus epoch||sequence to the DTLS 1.2 record protection path.
results.standard_section: RFC 6347 Section 4.1 Record Layer; Section 4.1.2.1 MAC
results.comparison_summary: requirement: must equal original ClientHello value for version under condition 'Client responds to a HelloVerifyRequest'; standard meaning: RFC 6347 Section 4.1 Record Layer; Section 4.1.2.1 MAC; code behavior: BoringSSL defines DTLS1_2_VERSION as 0xfefd, emits DTLS record headers through dtls_record_version, and supplies record_version plus epoch||sequence to the DTLS 1.2 record protection path.; conclusion: 满足.
